objective: Strategie de Momentum sur ATOMUSDC 4h. Indicateurs : MACD (histogram), ATR. Entree short quand MACD histogram decroissant et negatif. Stop-loss = 1.5x ATR, take-profit = 3.0x ATR.

iterations.hypothesis: Combining Bollinger Bands, ATR and MACD (histogram) provides a robust trading strategy as they all give different kinds of signals which when combined can lead to reduced false signals.
Combining the Bollinger band and MACD histogram together provides a better trading signal than using either one alone as they have different timeframes to capture trend direction.
Combining MACD histogram and ATR with a Bollinger breakout strategy reduces false signals in ranging markets while maintaining an edge from the trend-following ADX filter.

pre_reflection.text: [Pre-reflection] This strategy combines MACD histogram and ATR with Bollinger breakout to reduce false signals in ranging markets while maintaining an edge from the trend-following ADX filter. Weaknesses include sensitivity to noise, potential for whipsaws and lack of diversification. If results are poor, adjusting stop loss and take profit parameters could be tried. Mediocre Sharpe ratio suggests tweaking parameters to improve risk/reward balance. In case this strategy fails, a mean reversion approach or a different trend-following strategy may be considered.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This is an attempt to use MACD histogram and ATR as input for trade entry and exit in the ATOMUSDC 4h strategy. The current iteration uses close above macd.histogram, atr below 0.7*atr.middle, and macd.histogram crossing over macd.signal as conditions for entering a long position. Exits include either closing price crossing lower bollinger band or ADL<20 or exiting when the close is greater than macd.histogram. The strategy will be tested on historical data of 12198 bars.
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return), I would consider changes such as expanding atr ranges, lowering entry conditions for macd histogram and ATR, increasing position size, or using a different time frame. If returns are mediocre (low Sharpe ratio), I could try adjusting parameters like taking profits at larger multiples of atr, adding other indicators to the strategy, or refining stop-loss criteria.
